stories.headline: April crypto hacks hit $606M, worst month since Feb 2025 as DeFi TVL bleeds $13B
stories.tldr: April losses to crypto exploits topped $606M across 12 incidents, triggering a $13B DeFi TVL exodus in 48 hours.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Two single-signature failures vaporized more value than all of Q1 combined, exposing cross-chain key management as DeFi's structural weak point.
the_details: Drift Protocol drained for $285M on April 1 via fake-token oracle manipulation tied to a compromised admin key.
Kelp DAO lost $292M on April 19 when one forged signature minted rsETH across 20 chains.
DeFi TVL slid from ~$99B to ~$85B in 48 hours, the lowest reading in a year.

stories.headline: SEC clears DEX front-ends from broker-dealer rules in major DeFi green light
stories.tldr: SEC staff said April 13 it will not object to Covered User Interface Providers operating DEX front-ends without broker-dealer registration.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The carve-out hands U.S. DeFi builders the first clear path to ship interfaces for tokenized securities without registering as broker-dealers.
the_details: Statement applies to interfaces that prepare and submit transactions in crypto asset securities, subject to disclosure conditions.
Builds on the SEC-CFTC March 17 joint release establishing a 5-category token taxonomy.
Coalition of 100+ firms including Coinbase and Ripple is now pressing Senate Banking to mark up the CLARITY Act.

stories.headline: BTC pinned below $80K, but headed for best month in a year up 13%
stories.tldr: Bitcoin failed to break $80K as concentrated sell walls capped a rally fueled by ETF inflows and $5B in fresh USDT supply.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A monthly close above $77K confirms April as BTC's strongest run since 2025, but the $80K ceiling is now the line the FOMC week must clear.
the_details: Spot bitcoin ETFs logged a nine-day inflow streak with over $2B added in recent sessions.
USDT supply grew roughly $5B in April, restoring stablecoin liquidity that had drained in Q1.
BTC trading near $78,100 and ETH near $2,328 with FOMC, BoJ, ECB and BoE all setting rates this week.

stories.headline: BTC-dollar correlation slams to -0.90, most extreme inverse move since 2022
stories.tldr: Bitcoin's 30-day correlation with the U.S. Dollar Index plunged to -0.90, the deepest negative print in nearly four years.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: BTC is trading like a pure dollar hedge, which means the FOMC and Treasury issuance schedule, not crypto-native flows, will set price this week.
the_details: DXY weakness through April lined up almost 1:1 with BTC's climb from the mid-$60Ks to near $80K.
Hyperliquid whales flipped to their most aggressively net-long BTC positioning since early March.
Funding rates remain deeply negative, leaving room for a short squeeze if DXY breaks lower.

stories.headline: Hyperliquid HYPE buyback flywheel pulls 97% of revenue as TAO rips +47% YTD
stories.tldr: Hyperliquid is recycling 97% of protocol revenue into HYPE buybacks while Bittensor (TAO) sits +47% YTD on $43M Q1 AI services revenue.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: With altcoin season index at 39/100, capital is concentrating in protocols that generate hard cash flow rather than chasing rotation narratives.
the_details: Hyperliquid is now the largest revenue-generating crypto project outside stablecoin issuers.
Bittensor market cap pushed near $3.5B as Q1 AI services pulled in $43M in fees.
BTC dominance sits at 60% with the CMC Altcoin Season Index stuck in 'Bitcoin Season' territory.
